rules.name: Kick Rate
rules.explanation: Kicks per hour from the counting session.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How many kicks should I feel?
faq.answer: At least 10 movements in 2 hours is the standard guideline.

faq.question: When should I start counting?
faq.answer: Most providers recommend starting kick counts at 28 weeks.

faq.question: Is the Kick Count Tracker Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard pregnancy and parenting formulas trusted by expecting parents. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What kicks counted should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate kicks counted value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Kick Count Tracker Calculator?
sections.body: The Kick Count Tracker Calculator is a free online tool designed for expecting parents who need quick, accurate calculations in the pregnancy and parenting space. By entering your kicks counted, time taken, weeks pregnant, you get instant results including kicks per hour, status, action. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting kicks per hour right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In pregnancy and parenting, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by expecting parents worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to prepare for parenthood with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your kicks counted and need to find the right kicks per hour. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Kicks Counted
howToSteps.description: Type or select your kicks counted in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Time Taken (minutes)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your time taken in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Weeks Pregnant
howToSteps.description: Type or select your weeks pregnant in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your kicks per hour, status, action. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your kicks counted before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both kicks per hour and status — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to prepare for parenthood.
If you're unsure about your weeks pregnant, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
